comment.body: Could be a couple of 'odd' days coming up.  In my experience, you see how far down the futures got and figure it's an easy short only to have the market levitate for a couple of days.  Basically, trapped shorts provide bids.  And it seems too weird.  Takes a couple days to burn off the trapped shorts.  But we'll see.

comment.body: Honestly I like your analysis and I am glad I am a subscriber since day 1, I don't really care for the constant back patting.   Had the market rallied, you could have claimed you called it too, as you mentioned close levels that negated a confirmation of the reversion trade.  It honestly lessens excellent commentary when you consistently say "called it, nailed it" given there is always a high degree of ambiguity to your words which allow you to play both sides.

comment.body:
What does it mean when we’ve been trading above the centroid? I noticed today that the price rejected at her upside pivot. 

Coding the centroid at 5974.75 - 5961.44 zone, a very important pivotal area. Until 1.20pm, momentum can grind below this keeping vol low (vol up, would reverse the momentum), but after 1.20pm, it is a strong supportive area. 

To the downside, momentum should take out 5951.27, with target of 5910 (also a local pin).
